key_insights.insight: Information is now a weapon, and digital tools have become weaponized, a trend drastically different from the 20th century.
key_insights.quote: And it's more now moving towards other forms where you can now destabilize a government or a system of government or a society using a server farm and 20 peoplesitting in a room thousands of miles away...where information becomes weaponized,where digital tools become weaponized.

key_insights.insight: We are fighting wars of different kinds, now less kinetic warfare, especially among major powers.
key_insights.quote: I just think we're fighting wars of different kinds.I think this idea of kinetic warfare is less frequent, especially among the major powers.

key_insights.insight: A breakdown in civic literacy contributes to the polarization of society.
key_insights.quote: It's a breakdown of civic literacy, that we don't understand how governments work...And we lose the civic connection with our literal neighbors.

key_insights.insight: Understanding political persuasion, rhetoric and propaganda is crucial to protect yourself from misinformation.
key_insights.quote: And here's how you understand, and that involves understanding the use of rhetoric,the use of political persuasion or propaganda, and just so at least we can try to be better informed.

key_insights.insight: To prepare against crisis or any political conflict regardless of how you feel about it, the best thing to do is to manage. Educate yourself with how misinformation works to help you be better informed.
key_insights.quote: The best you can do is manage.And you manage by educating people as much as you canon how to, on understanding how rhetoricalmanipulative influence works, understanding what Aristotle taught us these techniques a very long time ago and they're still enforced today

actionable_advice.advice: Stay curious and active in your community
actionable_advice.how_to: Continuously ask questions, seek knowledge, and participate in local civic matters to make informed decisions and avoid apathy.

episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - WW3 Threat Assessment: The War Has Quietly Started & No One’s Trying to Stop It!
